violations.text: Rice was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC added date. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front and side, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings in kitchen, bar, and basement areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Inspector observed dead roach on trap in upper bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Prep unit lids located in the kitchen area were not in good repair (lids broken) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the prep table shelves in the kitchen area have an accumulation of droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The ice machines are lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026
The floors in the basement area has an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - hole in kitchen wall (corner near cutting boards) - hole in kitchen wall (kitchen entrance from dining room) - hole in lower bar wall near dishwasher - hole in lower bar near lowboy - hole in basement floor/wall near basement stairs - hole in basement wall near office - broken floor tiles in base area - ceiling tiles in basement area (missing) - ceiling in basement storage area near ice machines (ceiling coming down) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026
The basement floor in the food prep area is in poor repair (needs resealing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The hand-washing sinks in the restrooms (basement and special event bar) are lacking proper handwashing signage. PIC placed signs during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The hand-washing sinks in the special event bar, special event bar restroom, and basement restroom were lacking paper towels. PIC replaced during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Inspector observed the following: - sealed food containers with droppings on top. PIC discarded items. - ice in bar ice bin with debris. PIC discarded ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
Inspector observed glue trap with dead roaches in the special event bar area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings in basement, kitchen, and basement areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026
Inspector observed single service items stored in the basement with droppings. PIC disposed of items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
The following was observed in poor repair: - prep units (lids broken) - ice machine (lid cracked with exposed insulation Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - freezer chests (ice buildup) - kitchen prep table shelves (droppings) - bar lowboy units (door tracks and inside) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The drip line hose from beverage dispenser was observed not connected to a drain. Nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - hole in kitchen wall (near entrance from dining room) - hole in bar wall near dishwasher - hole in bar wall near lowboy - hole in basement floor/wall near stairs - missing ceiling tiles in basement area - broken floor tiles in bar area - ceiling in basement storage area near ice machines (ceiling coming down) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The basement floor is in poor repair (needs resealing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The floors (especially under/behind equipment) in the basement and bar areas have an accumulation of droppings debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (lemons) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The hand-washing sinks at the special event bar and basement restroom were lacking proper handwashing signage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The hand-washing sink in the two bars and basement restroom were lacking paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Strainer and beverage dispenser tube were observed in the hand-washing sink at the main bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed tube and strainer. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (lids in basement area) were observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
Inspector observed rice exceeding the 7 day date marking. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the special event entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Foil is used in the construction of prep table shelf liner in the kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The following items were observed in poor repair: - prep unit lids (broken) - ice machine lid (cracked and exposed insulation) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - freezer chests (ice buildup) - bar lowboy units (spills) - bar glassware coolers (broken glass and old fruit wedges) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - hole in kitchen wall (near entrance from dining room) - hole in bar wall near dishwasher - hole in bar wall near lowboy - hole in basement floor/wall near stairs - missing ceiling tiles in basement area - broken floor tiles in bar area - ceiling in basement storage area near ice machines (ceiling coming down) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Inspector observed a steady stream of water coming from ceiling in basement with water on prep table and basement floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The basement floor is in poor repair (needs resealing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The hand-washing sinks at the bar and restrooms were lacking proper handwashing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The hand-washing sinks in the basement bathroom and bar was lacking paper towels. PIC refilled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Inspector observed strainers in handwashing sinks in the bar areas. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC removed strainers. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The following food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food, droppings, and/or other debris: - bowls under prep table in kitchen - sheet tray shelf liners in plate storage area - can opener in kitchen area - lids and containers in basement area - ice machines in basement area The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took ice machines out of service, discarded ice, and placed other items in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
Inspector observed in-use utensils soaking in chlorine sanitizer. PIC informed to end practice and wash/sanitize utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Inspector observed vacuum sealer and ROP bags in basement. PIC discarded ROP bags, removed vacuum sealer from establishment, and discarded ROP cooked chicken. PIC informed they do not have approval to vacuum seal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The presence of rodents and small flies are not controlled as evidenced by small and large droppings, nesting material, gnaw marks, and live small flies. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the special event room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC placed items in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Foil is used as shelf liners under prep tables AND cardboard is used to line shelves. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The following was observed in poor repair: - ice machine lid (cracked and exposed insulation) - grill prep unit lid (broken) - lowboy in bar area (ambient temp 55F) - ice machine (not draining) New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink and dishwashers in kitchen and bars. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - prep units - prep tables/shelves - freezer chests (ice buildup) - low boy units - bar glassware cooler New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
Inspector observed a leak at the 3 bay sink faucet with a very loud high pitch noise. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2026
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The floor and wall junctures in the basement and kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement and kitchen areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The following was observed in poor repair. - hole in kitchen wall (near entrance from dining room) - hole in bar wall near dishwasher - hole in bar wall near lowboy - hole in basement floor/wall near stairs - missing ceiling tiles in basement area - broken floor tiles in bar area - ceiling in basement storage area near ice machines (ceiling coming down) The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
